notes.text:
From: MaryBeth Battalio <marybethb@smalley.com>  Sent: Friday, April 3, 2026 11:39 AM To: Rob Jeanquart <robj@smalley.com> Cc: Marina Shulman <marinas@smalley.com>; Frederick Briere
<fredob@smalley.com>; Ron Babajan <ronb@smalley.com>; Lauren Nagys
<laurenn@smalley.com> Subject: RE: Your opinion on States issue 69317 CRM - State field for
accounts outside of US One last bit of information… our CSI system is programed to know
when a state entry is required (likely due to SureShip requirement we’ve dealt
with in the past). This field in the Customers record is not a free type
field.  It can only be populated from a
drop down. … likely you know this already. I just spot checked A# 81173 on the list… Our system doesn’t
have a drop down option for Switzerland, but when I review on the internet this
client’s “canton” = Bern.Their POs do not outline this and these orders flowed through
SureShip and were successfully delivered without it.     MB  MaryBeth Battalio | marybethb@smalley.comSenior Manager, Customer ServiceSmalley | 555 Oakwood Road, Lake Zurich, IL 60047 | USAT: +1 (847) 719-5900 | Direct: +1 (847) 719-5915smalley.com | Facebook | LinkedIn | YouTube  From: Rob Jeanquart <robj@smalley.com>  Sent: Friday, April 3, 2026 11:18 AM To: MaryBeth Battalio <marybethb@smalley.com> Cc: Marina Shulman <marinas@smalley.com>;
Frederick Briere <fredob@smalley.com>;
Ron Babajan <ronb@smalley.com>;
Lauren Nagys <laurenn@smalley.com> Subject: FW: Your opinion on States issue 69317 CRM - State field for
accounts outside of US The attached should have some
data. Frederick / Marina / myself
spoke earlier – Frederick wanted some clarification on the problem.  We speculated on a solution but are not quite
sure.As of right now we’re going
to research using the existing ‘County’ field in SyteLine Customers for the
non-USA States. Plus we will bring up the subject to our consultant, Chris Weiss in a meeting
we have with him shortly. FYIRob  Rob Jeanquart | robj@smalley.comLead Software EngineerSmalley | 555 Oakwood Road, Lake Zurich, IL 60047 | USAT: +1 (847) 719-5900 Ext. 5724smalley.com | Facebook | LinkedIn | YouTube  From: MaryBeth Battalio
<marybethb@smalley.com>  Sent: Friday, April 3, 2026 11:08 AM To: Rob Jeanquart <robj@smalley.com>;
Frederick Briere <fredob@smalley.com>;
Frank Naugo <frankn@smalley.com>;
Lauren Nagys <laurenn@smalley.com> Cc: Marina Shulman <marinas@smalley.com>;
Ron Babajan <ronb@smalley.com> Subject: RE: Your opinion on States issue 69317 CRM - State field for
accounts outside of US HI Rob,The attached spreadsheet is blank.MB  MaryBeth Battalio | marybethb@smalley.comSenior Manager, Customer ServiceSmalley | 555 Oakwood Road, Lake Zurich, IL 60047 | USAT: +1 (847) 719-5900 | Direct: +1 (847) 719-5915smalley.com | Facebook | LinkedIn | YouTube  From: Rob Jeanquart <robj@smalley.com>  Sent: Friday, April 3, 2026 8:38 AM To: MaryBeth Battalio <marybethb@smalley.com>;
Frederick Briere <fredob@smalley.com>;
Frank Naugo <frankn@smalley.com>;
Lauren Nagys <laurenn@smalley.com> Cc: Marina Shulman <marinas@smalley.com>;
Ron Babajan <ronb@smalley.com> Subject: RE: Your opinion on States issue 69317 CRM - State field for
accounts outside of US +Lauren I’m going throw the Smalley
website and Pardot into the mix for a little flavor.I bet most of these non-valid
States are originating from Pardot. With that there is probably no to little
chance that anyone in Sales is editing the accounts created by Pardot before it
gets promoted to CSI-SyteLine to become a customer. So the idea to validate the
State in CRM would work some of the time for those being edited/added, the
remaining will slip through.  If they are being promoted because of a quote I can say that we will have
Quoting of Standards implemented in CRM soon, that will reduce this occurrence.If they are being promoted
for a Sample (or other types of quotes) then we may need to have a process in
place to alert the someone to edit the customer to correct an invalid State.Marina mentioned that the
invalid state shows up blank on the customer screen in SyteLine – here is an
example of one; I’ve attached a list of
customers with non-valid states along with the #of Estimates, #of Sample
Orders, #of Customer Orders. Rob  Rob Jeanquart | robj@smalley.comLead Software EngineerSmalley | 555 Oakwood Road, Lake Zurich, IL 60047 | USAT: +1 (847) 719-5900 Ext. 5724smalley.com | Facebook | LinkedIn | YouTube  From: MaryBeth Battalio
<marybethb@smalley.com>  Sent: Thursday, April 2, 2026 6:03 PM To: Rob Jeanquart <robj@smalley.com>;
Frederick Briere <fredob@smalley.com>;
Frank Naugo <frankn@smalley.com> Cc: Marina Shulman <marinas@smalley.com>;
Ron Babajan <ronb@smalley.com> Subject: RE: Your opinion on States issue 69317 CRM - State field for
accounts outside of US Hi Rob, I wanted to share a bit of context from the Customer Service
side that might help as we think through the best solution. Many of the accounts promoted to CSI are usually being set up
for quoting purposes, so they are considered Prospect accounts rather than
Active accounts. In practice, Sales & Marketing is responsible for these
promotions, not Customer Service.  For
this reason, I don’t recommend prompting CS to fix Prospect accounts that are
being created for quotes or sample orders, since CS typically isn’t involved at
that stage.  If an issue is detected, the
“user” who created the record should be flagged, if that’s an available option. Ideally, CRM should be configured to mirror the data validation
rules in CSI.  CS works only with Active accounts, or with Prospect accounts
once we receive their first order. If we’re the ones promoting an account or
working with an already promoted one, our CSP-100 procedure requires us to “set
the account up” to an active state and fix any anomalies at that time.  If there are any accounts in this list that
are actually “active,” that would indicate my team isn’t following our CSP-100
process, and I’d want to know about those.  If the STATUS field on the CSI Customers record Main Tab is BLANK
= prospect.  There are also times when prospect accounts are set up and
sample orders are shipped rapidly—sometimes so quickly that a report wont alert
anyone before shipping is completed. In these cases, CS isn’t involved and the
CSP-100 procedure isn’t applied.  I’m
would think that these scenarios are causing problems with the flow from CSI to
SureShip, when it comes to shipping orders and those usually come back to CS
for review & fixing. One important rule we do have is to avoid special
characters—such as é or 府—in all fields, since these don’t flow through SureShip. Could you clarify how many of the problematic accounts are
actually “active” order accounts? If there are any, I definitely need to
address that within my team.  I am happy
to collaborate further to find a solution that works for all teams. Thanks again for raising this and for your ongoing support! Best,MaryBeth  MaryBeth Battalio | marybethb@smalley.comSenior Manager, Customer ServiceSmalley | 555 Oakwood Road, Lake Zurich, IL 60047 | USAT: +1 (847) 719-5900 | Direct: +1 (847) 719-5915smalley.com | Facebook | LinkedIn | YouTube  From: Rob Jeanquart <robj@smalley.com>  Sent: Thursday, April 2, 2026 5:04 PM To: Frederick Briere <fredob@smalley.com>;
Frank Naugo <frankn@smalley.com>;
MaryBeth Battalio <marybethb@smalley.com> Cc: Marina Shulman <marinas@smalley.com>;
Ron Babajan <ronb@smalley.com> Subject: Your opinion on States issue 69317 CRM - State field for
accounts outside of US Hi Frederick/Frank/MaryBeth, There is an issue with States
as they get promoted from CRM to SyteLine:CRM allows to free type a
  state code without performing a validation. The State field is allowing to
  enter up to 64 char. When an account gets promoted to CSI, this field is
  truncated to 5 char creating invalid data in CSI.This is not a problem with
  US Accounts but, it is for the account outside of US and Canada. Some CSI
  forms validate this data (customer complaints) and as result cannot process
  the record.  Here is a current list of
non-valid states in production SyteLine:GujaGyeoKarnLiedSASPTIAN12350160103149256010Abu DABUDHAKLAlabaAlajuAlexaAleyAndhrANKARArizoArkanATLANAucklAUKAyuttBac NBajaBANGABangkbanglBeijiBengabinhBogotBusanCa.CalifCANTECApeCasadÇAYIRcentaCenteCentrChachCHIHUCHNchonbChungClareCoahuCoimbCotaCPCundiDOHADongDOUBSDUBAIDubliEastEL TEEsEXFloriGauteGENOVGeorgGoaGoiásGuangGUJARGwangGyeonGYEOUHaifaHanoiHaryaHo ChHONGIdahoIncheIndiaisraeJakarJalisJerusJiangJinagJingsJOHORKAKARNAKEDAHKentKERALKONYAKulaiKulimKyeonKZNLAGUNLimaLouisMaharMANAMManilMarylMassaMendoMercaMéxicMichiMICHOMinasMinneMiranMissoMorelMumbaMurciN TN.L.NANAGASNaviNerimNevadNew DNew JNew SNew YNLNONTHNorthNRWNuevoOhioONTAROregoOtagoPahanPalghPardePenanPerthPueblPulauPuneQro.QueenQuereRAJASRajkoRayonRhodeRio dRJROGOZRotorRSSaitaSan LSantaSantiSao PSão PSELANSelecseongShaanShandShangSharjShenzSingaSLPSonorSPTainaTamauTAMILTAMPTampsTanziTELANTenneTerenTexasTianTianjTiruvTUASUmm aUniteUPUtahUttarVICTOVietVirgiWashiWESTWesteWiscoZacatZhe jZheJi大阪府 Some of these could be
converted to actual / valid states.  I
could see us identifying those and update the customers to the valid state ids. Options might be:   1) Create the States (I don’t advise this) 2) Fix the problem on the customer when the issue comes up – at the time an
order is taken or when the country gets validated. Like the saying goes; “Don’t
trouble trouble until trouble troubles you”.3) Setup an alert when a
customer with this situation gets created to have the system send an email to
someone in Customer Service to take care it before it is a problem.  My thought was first to convert those that might be U.S. States and then setup
a rule (#3)  Your thoughts?? Thanks, Rob

notes.text:
Reached out to Frank to let him about the possibility of using Edge browser to edit notes: Hi Frank, Hope all is well. I am reaching out after reviewing a
support ticket that was submitted and wanted to see if it is possible for you
and the team that currently are in need of editing capabilities to use Edge
browser as an option in the interim. We are currently out of licenses and are looking for
efficient ways to allow you to use the Trip Take Along Report without the need
for a licensed version of Adobe. Please let me know your thoughts. Here is a snippet of the Notes section being edited with
Edge browser:  Thanks,Ron

Frank reached out:Hi Ron,This seems like a
possibility.  Let me run it by a few of the guys and see about it.
 Thank you,
